full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic docking-adapter floor per the NASA 'Spacecraft Docking Adapter with Flexible but Load-Bearing Floor' open-CAD challenge. The floor is the walkable interface between a docked crew vehicle and a station module. Geometric constraints per the brief: floor accommodates a standing spacesuited astronaut footprint of 1.0 m x 0.78 m; each boot footprint 0.39 m x 0.24 m; total usable floor depth (floor-to-ceiling) at least 16 in (406 mm) below the docking tunnel flange; floor circular OD 1.6 m with a central 0.8 m diameter docking-hatch opening. Material: aluminum-lithium alloy 2195 (E = 78 GPa, yield = 540 MPa, ultimate = 590 MPa, density = 2710 kg/m^3). Floor thickness to be sized. Mounted around perimeter to the docking tunnel with 24 bolted lugs (M8 Grade A2-70 stainless). Load cases: (R1) Single-boot astronaut crash: 2.5 x astronaut-plus-suit mass (160 kg) = 400 kg lumped through a single boot footprint 0.39 x 0.24 m, = 3,924 N over 0.0936 m^2 = 42 kPa patch load; max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 360 MPa; floor deflection at load <= 5 mm. (R2) Two-boot standing: full astronaut 160 kg through two 0.39 x 0.24 m patches = 1,570 N total, 8.4 kPa each patch; max von Mises <= 360 MPa; deflection <= 2 mm. (R3) Docking flange pressurized load: cabin pressure 1 atm (101.3 kPa) acting on the full 1.6 m diameter floor area from above = 203,700 N total (2.01 m^2 x 101.3 kPa); max von Mises <= yield / 1.25 = 432 MPa; deflection <= 15 mm. (R4) Floor flexibility: a quasi-static 500 N uniform load across the full walking area must produce a measurable but bounded deflection (target 5-15 mm) so the floor 'gives' slightly under load (challenge 'flexible but load-bearing' objective). (R5) Floor mass <= 35 kg. (R6) First modal frequency fixed at perimeter >= 20 Hz. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3 pass/fail cases + R4 flexibility range) + modal (R6). Vibration secondary via R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid floor, perimeter bolt drawing, and a report listing floor mass in kg, max von Mises per case in MPa, deflection per case in mm, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic docking-adapter floor per the NASA 'Spacecraft Docking Adapter with Flexible but Load-Bearing Floor' open-CAD challenge. The floor is the walkable interface between a docked crew vehicle and a station module.

Envelope: circular 1.6 m OD with an 0.8 m central hatch opening, depth >= 406 mm (16 in) below the tunnel flange, accommodating a standing spacesuited astronaut footprint of 1.0 m x 0.78 m with 0.39 x 0.24 m boots. Perimeter attachment via 24 M8 lugs.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 for crash/standing cases, yield FoS 1.25 for the pressurized case; the brief also asks for a bounded 'flexible but load-bearing' deflection target.

Load cases.
- Single-boot crash: 400 kg (2.5 x 160 kg suited astronaut) through one 0.39 x 0.24 m boot = 42 kPa patch (3,924 N).
- Two-boot standing: 160 kg through two 0.39 x 0.24 m patches = 8.4 kPa per patch.
- Docking flange pressurized: 1 atm (101.3 kPa) across the full 1.6 m OD floor (~203.7 kN total).
- Flexibility: 500 N uniform UDL across the walking area, target deflection 5-15 mm.
- Fixed-perimeter first-mode frequency >= 20 Hz.

prompt.title: NASA spacecraft docking floor
prompt.brief: Al-Li 2195 circular floor, 1.6 m OD with 0.8 m central hatch. Flexible but load-bearing, survives boot crash + pressurized cabin differential.

boundary_conditions.loads: patch or uniform pressure on floor

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Pressure differential reduced to uniform pressure.

eval_coverage: flexibility range (upper and lower deflection bound)
